actions.label: Address NVTS breakdown
actions.detail: NVTS is the flagged worst-drawdown position at -11.3% with RSI14 23.6, price below both MA20 ($20.55) and MA50 ($21.28). Operating margin -195%, net margin -255% — this is a cash-burning semis name, not a mispriced quality asset. No IR/residual-alpha data resolved, so there is no alpha claim to make here; oversold reading alone is not a buy signal without a fundamental catalyst. Position is small (2.7% weight, $304.60), so the dollar risk is contained, but continuing to hold through further breakdown adds tail risk for no evidenced edge. Net edge of holding here is negative — friction-adjusted, trimming to a token position or exiting on any close below $13.50 clears risk cheaply given the small size.

actions.label: Trim MU into strength
actions.detail: MU is up 323.74%, the largest gain and largest position in the book ($2,227.19, tax_sensitive_winner=true). Memory is a cyclical, mean-reverting commodity business — P/FCF has stretched to 192x versus P/CF of 18.3x, showing capex is eating free cash flow even as reported margins look fine (27% operating margin). No IR data resolved, so this is purely a valuation/cycle-position call, not alpha. A full sale realizes a large long-term gain and a real tax bill; the better structure is a partial trim (20-30% of the position) to lock in cycle gains while keeping upside exposure, or a collar (sell calls near $1,050-1,100, buy protective puts near $900) to defer the tax event while capping downside. Net edge: trimming into a 192x P/FCF print clearly outweighs the after-tax drag on a partial sale; a full outright sale's edge is thinner once taxes are modeled — collar is the preferred structure here.

actions.label: Trim/hedge VRT — re-bucket as AI-capex beta, not a diversifier
actions.detail: VRT is up 79.85% and sits below MA50 ($324.82) while the broader semis complex (NVDA, MRVL) is already showing technical weakness. Vertiv's revenue is directly tied to AI data-center buildout capex — it moves with the same factor as AMD/NVDA/TSM/MU, so it does not diversify the book despite sitting in a different sector template label. Fundamentals are fine (18.5% operating margin) but the 47.5x P/E leaves no valuation cushion if the capex cycle cools. No IR/alpha data resolved — this is a beta-concentration call. Net edge: trimming 15-20% of the position to reduce correlated AI-capex exposure is worth the modest tax drag on the gain, given the book is already carrying MU, TSM, AMD, NVDA, MRVL in the same factor.

actions.label: Redeploy cash toward SOFI/financials to widen the factor base
actions.detail: With $1,277.70 cash plus a $2,000 pending deposit (about 22% of equity in dry powder), the highest-value use is closing the factor gap rather than adding a fifth semis name. SOFI is the only clean rate/credit exposure in the book, up 10.38% and trading above both MA20 ($17.33) and MA50 ($16.87) with RSI14 69.6 reflecting positioning strength, not exhaustion, under the financials template. No IR data resolved so this is not an alpha claim — it's a diversification-by-factor decision. Net edge: adding to a genuinely uncorrelated sleeve when effective independent bets are running near 3-4 despite 12 nominal positions has a clear risk-reduction edge that outweighs the opportunity cost of not adding to the already-crowded semis trade.
